original_abstract: Since its inception in 1951, the k-Nearest Neighbors (kNN) algorithm has evolved into a cornerstone of modern data mining, recommendation engines, and Internet of Things (IoT) applications. This paper provides a comprehensive review and performance analysis of advancements in exact kNN techniques, with a specific focus on kNN Search and kNN Join operations within high-dimensional spaces. We conduct a deep dive into 31 search methods and 12 join methods, offering a methodological overview and critical insights into their respective strengths, limitations, and practical utility. To support reproducibility and further experimentation, we provide the source code for every method discussed. Driven by the increasing complexity of high-dimensional data and the lack of exhaustive surveys on exact kNN approaches, this work seeks to fill a critical gap in the literature. Furthermore, we identify persistent challenges and propose future research directions, providing a holistic guide that synthesizes, compares, and evaluates the current state of the art in kNN methodology.
humanized_abstract: Since 1951, the k-Nearest Neighbors (kNN) algorithm has become one of the main data mining, recommendation systems and Internet of Things (IoT) tools. This work makes a review and evaluation of the kNN search and kNN Join methods and tools used to get efficient results of kNN algorithm in high dimensional spaces. In the light of literature survey made in this work, which includes 31 search methods and 12 join methods in high dimensional spaces with providing an implementation for all of them, we explain the underlying concepts of those methods and discuss their properties and applicable fields. They also present a list of open problems and suggestions regarding this issue in order to have a pioneer work which reviews, compares and evaluates the kNN methods existing in the literature and direct further research works.
